mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining FEDFUNDS rate over the trailing three months signals that the Federal Reserve has paused its tightening cycle, reducing discount-rate pressure on equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 2.4% since December 2018 after Fed signaled pause
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return frequency in flat/declining FEDFUNDS periods is not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window from cycle lows, it signals an inflection in the labor market that historically precedes earnings deceleration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over trailing 3 months from a cycle low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% in Feb 2019, near cycle lows
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or Rank IC is not significantly different from zero at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in RSAFS retail sales growth and a decline in HOUST housing starts jointly signal a broad-based softening in consumer and residential demand that is not captured by either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND HOUST declines > 5% over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1226K revised, showing softening from 2018 highs
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce positive 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed the average of the two individual-series Rank ICs by a meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when industrial production growth is decelerating but still positive, INDPRO deceleration is a leading indicator of manufacturing slowdown that markets underprice in the very short term, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as high-frequency traders react to data releases.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value showed slowing momentum into early 2019
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following INDPRO releases show no significant negative skew (t-test p > 0.10) or Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds the trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate by more than 1 percentage point (a restrictive real rate regime), tightening financial conditions compound over subsequent quarters, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.4% vs CPIAUCSL YoY near 1.5-2.0% in early 2019
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: Triggered restrictive-real-rate periods show 60-day forward SPX returns positive more than 50% of the time, or Rank IC not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong PAYEMS growth alongside stable or falling UNRATE indicates a tightening labor market that raises unit labor costs and compresses margins with a lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both signals move jointly beyond typical late-cycle thresholds.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 150,606K with UNRATE at 3.8% in Feb 2019
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods produce positive 20-day forward SPX returns in over 60% of instances, or interaction Rank IC does not outperform either single-series baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in HOUST housing starts reflects durable consumer confidence and construction-sector demand, which correlates with short-horizon equity momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trend and 5-day forward SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing regime (FEDFUNDS flat or declining), a rebound in INDPRO growth signals reacceleration of the supply side that combines with lower financing costs to disproportionately boost equity returns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this effect is regime-specific and does not hold during tightening cycles.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns positive after two negative months
evidence_refs: fact:Fed signaled pause in early 2019 while INDPRO growth was volatile
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS pause/easing periods, INDPRO rebound signal fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).
